title: Peppery Chewy Brazilian Beef Stew
description: A hearty, slow‑cooked stew loaded with bold pepper flavors, tender beef cubes, and a comforting ketchup‑based sauce that’s as energizing as it is satisfying.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium‑high heat.
Add the beef cubes and sear on all sides until a brown crust forms, about 5 minutes.
Transfer the seared beef to a slow cooker or Dutch oven.
In the same skillet, sauté the onion, garlic, and bell peppers until softened, about 3 minutes.
Pour the sautéed vegetables over the beef and stir in ketchup, tamari, smoked paprika, and cayenne pepper.
Add the beef broth and bay leaf, and bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Cover and cook on low for 4 hours (or on high for 2 hours) until the beef is melt‑in‑your‑mouth tender.
Remove the bay leaf, taste, and season with salt and pepper as needed.
Serve hot over rice, polenta, or crusty bread, and enjoy the bold, peppery flavors.
